title: H-10b secret-commit gate has no integration test — only regex unit coverage
evidence: test_hook_guards.py exercises H-09b (crypto commit) end-to-end with a real staged diff in a throwaway git repo (cases 6a–6e). The H-10b branch in pre-bash.py (lines 316–341) shares the same code block — it fires when SECRET_RE matches instead of CRYPTO_RE — but no integration test stages a file containing a SECRET_RE-matching literal (e.g. `password = "abcd1234"`) and asserts that `git commit` is blocked with `[H-10b]`. SECRET_RE is unit-tested in test_hooklib.py (SecretReTest), but the end-to-end path through pre-bash.py — diff extraction -> SECRET_RE scan -> marker check -> block — is not exercised. A regex gap or code path divergence in H-10b (e.g. the `kind`/`tag`/`skill` selection branch) would be invisible.
impact: A bug specific to the H-10b code path (wrong tag, wrong skill name, wrong block condition for secret lines) would not be caught before release. The gate would appear to pass CI while silently failing to block secret commits — defeating the product's core value proposition on the secret-handling lane.
recommendation: Add integration test cases to test_hook_guards.py that stage a SECRET_RE-matching file (e.g. a line containing `api_key = "abcd1234"`) and assert: (a) no marker → blocks with [H-10b]; (b) empty marker → blocks on coverage; (c) genuine security-pass.py run → admits; (d) TOCTOU: different secret line added after pass → re-blocks. Mirror the H-09b structure exactly.
acceptance_criteria: A new test case in test_hook_guards.py stages a SECRET_RE-matching line and asserts exit 2 + '[H-10b]' in stderr
A TOCTOU case for H-10b (new secret line added after a recorded pass) is present
The full suite continues to pass

title: git commit -a with crypto lines: H-09b/H-10b worktree-diff branch untested in integration
evidence: pre-bash.py lines 313–315: `if COMMIT_ALL_RE.search(commit.group('args')) or add: added += '\n' + added_lines(cwd, 'HEAD')`. This extends the sensitive-line scan to the full worktree diff when `git commit -a` (or `-am`) is used. The H-09b integration tests (6a–6e) only test plain `git commit -m` with staged files. No test case stages a crypto change in the INDEX-only form and then issues `git commit -am` to prove that the worktree branch is hit and that it blocks. The H-14 migration backstop test (OB-10 in test_migration_backstop.py) does cover the `-a` path for migration detection, confirming the pattern exists, but the security-gate analog is absent.
impact: A regression in the `COMMIT_ALL_RE` branch (e.g. the regex failing to match `-am`) would mean `git commit -am` could ship crypto/secret changes without a gate pass, because `added_lines(cwd, '--cached')` alone would return empty if the file is not explicitly staged.
recommendation: Add test case 6f to the H-09b block in test_hook_guards.py: add a crypto line to the worktree (do NOT `git add`), then run `git commit -am 'sneak via -a'` and assert exit 2 + '[H-09b]' in stderr. Mirror OB-10's fixture approach from test_migration_backstop.py.
acceptance_criteria: A test case in test_hook_guards.py issues `git commit -am` with a worktree-only crypto change and asserts block [H-09b]
The full suite continues to pass

title: validateRepoUrl: scp-like URL edge cases not covered (double-colon host::path, numeric-prefix user)
evidence: validateRepoUrl's scp-like allow-path is `/^[A-Za-z0-9._-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+:[^:].*$/`. The `: [^:]` guard is intended to block the transport-helper `host::address` form. create.test.ts does not assert that `git@github.com::path` (double-colon) is rejected, nor that a username beginning with a digit is rejected (the current regex `[A-Za-z0-9._-]+` would accept `1user@host:path` but git may not). More critically, there is no negative test for `git@github.com::evil-command` — a double-colon at position 16 passes the `[^:]` guard because the checked position is the char AFTER the first colon (which is `:`, a second colon → match fails correctly) but this is subtle enough that a regex edit could re-introduce the hole. The test suite only covers three accept cases (https, ssh://, scp-like) and four reject classes (leading `-`, transport-helpers, empties, non-network schemes). The `host::address` double-colon case is not explicitly listed.
impact: If the scp-like regex were weakened (e.g. changing `[^:]` to `.`), a `host::address` transport-helper form would pass validation and reach git's argv in a networked clone container. An explicit negative test would have caught the 2026-06-22 security pass note about this exact guard.
recommendation: Add negative test cases to create.test.ts: assert `validateRepoUrl('git@github.com::evil')` throws InvalidRepoUrlError; assert `validateRepoUrl('git@github.com:/path')` is accepted (single colon, valid); assert `validateRepoUrl('git@github.com:path/sub')` is accepted.
acceptance_criteria: create.test.ts contains a test that asserts `git@github.com::evil` (double-colon) is rejected
The full vitest suite continues to pass

title: scope_globs custom extend/exclude path: no unit test for is_ci_path / is_deploy_path with custom declarations
evidence: test_hooklib.py's PathGlobTest exercises is_migration_path, is_ci_path, and is_deploy_path only against the DEFAULT globs (no security-controls.md present). test_migration_backstop.py extends this to test the extend/exclude grammar (`+ glob`, `- glob`) for is_migration_path specifically (OB-02, OB-03). But the `scope_globs` + `path_in_globs` plumbing used by is_ci_path and is_deploy_path is NOT tested with custom declarations — no test writes a `ci-paths` or `deploy-paths` block to security-controls.md and verifies the detection is extended or narrowed. The code path that parses _CI_DECL_RE and _DEPLOY_DECL_RE in scope_globs (lines 277–293) is dead from a test perspective.
impact: A bug in the CI/deploy-path declaration parsing (wrong regex, off-by-one in the `+ /-` grammar) would go undetected. While H-15/H-16 are advisory (non-blocking), a broken scope means the advisory never fires on custom CI/deploy paths — eliminating the reminder before a vulnerable workflow ships.
recommendation: Add test cases to test_hooklib.py's PathGlobTest: write a temporary security-controls.md with a `<!-- ci-paths -->+ custom/ci/**<!-- /ci-paths -->` block and assert is_ci_path matches; do the same with an exclude. Mirror OB-02/OB-03 from test_migration_backstop.py for the CI and deploy scopes.
acceptance_criteria: test_hooklib.py contains at least one test for custom ci-paths extend/exclude via security-controls.md
test_hooklib.py contains at least one test for custom deploy-paths extend/exclude via security-controls.md
The full suite continues to pass

title: post-write-edit H-12 reminder: no integration test for the full hook path (governs_index -> remind)
evidence: test_governs.py unit-tests governs_index() in isolation (cache hits/misses, ADR filtering, glob parsing). test_post_write_edit_scope.py drives the whole post-write-edit.py hook over stdin for H-15/H-16/H-17 but contains NO test for H-12. The integration path — write an ADR with a `governs:` field, then write a file that matches the glob, run post-write-edit.py, assert 'H-12' in stderr — is not exercised. A regression in the fnmatch call or the reminder dispatch (e.g. wrong stderr channel, missing `rel` computation) would be invisible.
impact: Low: H-12 is advisory; a broken reminder does not allow a guard bypass. However it eliminates the early warning that is the feature's purpose.
recommendation: Add one integration test to test_post_write_edit_scope.py: seed an ADR with `governs: src/*.ts` in the temp repo, write a file at `src/app.ts` via the hook payload, and assert 'H-12' in stderr and exit 0.
acceptance_criteria: test_post_write_edit_scope.py contains a test that drives post-write-edit.py and asserts [H-12] in stderr for a governed path
A non-governed path does NOT emit [H-12]
